findings.problem: The closing names the chapter's thesis in abstract terms (redemption/labor/hope), which reduces ambiguity and lets the narrator interpret the moment for the reader instead of leaving tension active.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:354, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:356, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:358. Keep Eli's shift legible through physical behavior, setting detail, and choice under fatigue, but remove explicit thematic labeling so the scene consequence carries the meaning.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised closing span anchored to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:354, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:356, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:358 conveys Eli's changed stance through concrete action/image and does not include direct abstract thesis statements; FAIL if the narration still states the moral in explicit summary form.

findings.problem: Timidity in aftermath handling: once EMS exits, the text pivots quickly to cleanup/reporting and under-renders immediate human fallout, softening the full social consequence of the overdose event.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:124, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:126, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:128, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:132. Add one embodied aftermath beat (coworker destabilization, contamination burden, or Eli's stress crash) that causally bridges the rescue to Kline's report demand.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised post-EMS section includes at least one on-page consequence beat that changes Eli's immediate behavior before the statement-writing starts; FAIL if the sequence still cuts from stretcher departure straight to administrative procedure with no intervening embodied fallout.

findings.problem: Kline's speech stays uniformly policy-formal across all turns, creating stilted over-formality and narrowing her to an institutional mouthpiece rather than a pressured person with register variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:132, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:134, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:146, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:158, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:306. Preserve authority but introduce one contraction and one concrete, scene-specific line that is not legal/policy boilerplate.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Kline's revised exchange contains at least one contraction and one non-policy, scene-anchored sentence while still enforcing compliance; FAIL if every line remains fully bureaucratic command language.

findings.problem: The Eli-Theo terms sequence leans on repeated one-word assent ("Done/Fair/Okay"), producing transcript cadence and reducing leverage shifts in a high-stakes exchange.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:236, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:240, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:242, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:246, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:250. Keep Theo's boundaries but give Eli at least two substantive responses that show friction, ownership, or negotiation in his own register.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Eli has at most one one-word assent in the revised terms span and at least two replies longer than four words that add pressure-relevant content; FAIL if his acceptance still reads as repeated minimal acknowledgments.

findings.problem: A recurring comma-then transition pattern ("..., then ...") appears repeatedly and starts to flatten rhythmic variation, signaling autopilot sentence construction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:50, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:52, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:54, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:158, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:304, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:342. Rebuild transitions with mixed structures (causal clauses, interruptions, hard stops, or beat breaks) rather than repeating comma-then linkage.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised targeted lines contain no more than two comma-then transitions total and show at least three distinct transition structures by inspection; FAIL if the same comma-then sequencing remains dominant in those spans.

findings.problem: Minor-character introductions repeatedly rely on accessory inventory (bracelet/hearing aid/watch/tattoo/tins/jacket), creating mechanical repetition from over-literal concreteness and flattening differentiation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:66, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:84, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:104, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:132, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:330. Keep one high-yield concrete detail per entrance, then prioritize behavior, syntax, or interactional pressure for characterization.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least three targeted character entrances are revised to one concrete object detail plus one behavioral cue (instead of stacked accessory tags); FAIL if most of those entrances still depend on multi-item object cataloging.

summary: Chapter 15 lands core plot obligations and stays causally coherent, but it does not clear pass-level quality: thematic over-explication in the coda, compressed immediate aftermath, dialogue register/cadence flattening in key exchanges, and repeated prose formulas reduce impact.
